justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet & Driver Management, Delivery Fulfillment, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out — e.g., route recalculation triggered by delivery state changes, vehicle class as a cross-context constraint between Fleet and Route Planning. Vehicle class correctly marked invariant: true (FD-1), stop time correctly marked invariant: false (RP-5/RP-6), notification window treated as non-invariant. 7 core Given/When/Then acceptance criteria plus extensive edge cases. All 3 recalculation triggers correctly enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation).

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough bounded context separation with clear responsibility boundaries and a well-structured table format
Cross-context dependencies surfaced as integration points — explicitly notes notification should react to domain events, not couple to route internals
Invariant classification is precise: vehicle class (hard invariant), recalculation triggers (invariant), stop time (soft default), notification window (non-invariant)
Goes beyond the rubric with insightful critical observations — especially the distinction between notification as promise vs status update
Edge cases are exceptionally well-chosen: rapid-fire recalculation debouncing, multi-driver zone conflicts, vehicle breakdown mid-shift
Rule FD-3 (vehicle class determined by shift vehicle, not just license) shows deep domain reasoning beyond what the spec likely stated
Route recalculation triggers marked as invariant: Yes, but initial route generation on shift start marked invariant: No — slight inconsistency in classification rationale
Confidence assessment with explicit low-confidence areas gives MJ and Shaq clear guidance on what needs clarification before implementation

# Domain Analysis: Smart Route Optimization

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This feature encodes the **last-mile delivery orchestration process** — the critical handoff between warehouse/hub operations and the customer's doorstep. It spans the moment a driver begins their shift through the completion (or failure) of each delivery attempt.
